# Team News: Spain vs. Saudi Arabia injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Matt Law| 20 Jun 2026 17:00, Last updated: 20 Jun 2026 17:00

© Iconsport / Aflo

Spain and Saudi Arabia will continue their respective 2026 World Cup campaigns with a clash in Group H on Sunday.

Saudi Arabia are second in the section on one point, while Spain are third, also on one point, and here Sports Mole rounds up the team news for the game.

---

## SPAIN VS. SAUDI ARABIA

https://www.sportsmole.co.uk/football/spain/world-cup-2026/preview/spain-vs-saudi-arabia-prediction-team-news-lineups_599589.html

## SPAIN

Out: None

Doubtful: Victor Munoz (hamstring)

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Simon; Llorente, Cubarsi, Laporte, Cucurella; Ruiz, Rodri, Pedri; Yamal, Oyarzabal, N Williams

## SAUDI ARABIA

Out: None

Doubtful: None

Sports Mole's predicted XI: Al-Owais; Abdulhamid, Al Tambakti, Al-Amri, Al Harbi; Al Shamat, Al-Khaibari, Kanno, S Al-Dawsari; Al Buraikan, Al-Juwayr

Written by

Matt Law

Share this article:

# Spain vs Saudi Arabia Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group H

Spain vs Saudi Arabia clash in Atlanta for their 2026 World Cup Group H Matchday 2 game. Predicted lineups, team news, tactical breakdown and score prediction.

June 19, 2026

Share

By:

Pierre Courtin

Adam Zdroik

June 19, 2026

World Cup

From the latest news to creating the best starting lineup, we've got you covered - Sign-up today

# Spain vs Saudi Arabia Preview: Predicted Lineups, Team News & Tactical Analysis | 2026 World Cup Group H

Spain did not get the start they expected. Held to a 0-0 draw by World Cup debutants Cape Verde, with 27 shots and nothing to show for it, the European champions head to Mercedes-Benz Stadium in Atlanta needing a win and a tidier finishing touch. Saudi Arabia, meanwhile, took a creditable point off Uruguay and will fancy this is the kind of game where a low block and one moment can spring a shock.

Group H is wide open. All four teams sit on a single point, so the math is simp

Spain XI vs Saudi Arabia: Lamine Yamal injury latest, confirmed team news and predicted lineup for World Cup 2026

Lamine Yamal has admitted that he is not yet ready to play a full 90 minutes as Spain prepare to face Saudi Arabia in the second of their World Cup 2026 group stage games.

The teenage star came into this tournament with a hamstring problem that ruled him out of the remainder of the club campaign, but he returned to action for his country in their shock 0-0 draw with Cape Verde in their opener.

Advertisement

Advertisement

He came on on the right flank for Luis de la Fuente’s side, but was given just 20 minutes to make an impact as the European champions were frustrated by the minnows.

Now, the Barcelona star has issued an update on his fitness ahead of taking on the

news_items.title: Spain vs Saudi Arabia at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN
news_items.author: Sunaadh Sagar
news_items.text:
Spain vs Saudi Arabia at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

Spain look to get their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign back on track when they take on Saudi Arabia in a Group H encounter at Atlanta.

Luis de la Fuente's side bore plenty of criticism after a 0-0 draw with Cape Verde in their World Cup opener, but have been channeling the spirit of 2010, when Spain lost their first match en-route winning the World Cup.

Saudi Arabia, meanwhile, raised hopes of qualification to the knockout stages after a 1-1 draw with Uruguay.

Here's everything you need to know about Monday's game.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on BBC One in the UK, Fox Sports in the U.S., Zee5 in India and SBS in Australia. You can also follow ESPN's live updates.

## Key Details:

Date, kick-off time: U.S. ET: Sunday June 21, 12 p.m. UK BST: Sunday June 21, 5 p.m. India IST: Sunday June 21, 9.30 p.m. Australia AEST: Monday June 22, 2 a.m.

Venue: Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta, USA Referee: Raphael Claus (Brazil)

## Team News and Predicted line-ups:

Spain

De la Fuente is still sweatin

# Team News: Spain vs. Saudi Arabia injury, suspension list, predicted XIs

By Matt Law| 20 Jun 2026 17:00, Last updated: 20 Jun 2026 17:00

© Iconsport / Aflo

Spain and Saudi Arabia will continue their respective 2026 World Cup campaigns with a clash in Group H on Sunday.

Saudi Arabia are second in the section on one point, while Spain are third, also on one point, and here Sports Mole rounds up the team news for the game.

Spain XI vs Saudi Arabia: Lamine Yamal injury latest, confirmed team news and predicted lineup for World Cup 2026

Lamine Yamal has admitted that he is not yet ready to play a full 90 minutes as Spain prepare to face Saudi Arabia in the second of their World Cup 2026 group stage games.

The teenage star came into this tournament with a hamstring problem that ruled him out of the remainder of the club campaign, but he returned to action for his country in their shock 0-0 draw with Cape Verde in their opener.

Advertisement

Advertisement

He came on on the right flank for Luis de la Fuente’s side, but was given just 20 minutes to make an impact as the European champions were frustrated by the minnows.

{"title": "Spain vs Saudi Arabia at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN", "url": "https://www.espn.com/soccer/story/_/id/49118145/fifa-world-cup-2026-spain-vs-saudi-arabia-tv-channel-how-watch-kickoff-live-stream-referee-predicted-lineups", "publishedDate": "2026-06-20T08:45:00.000Z", "author": "Sunaadh Sagar", "text": "Spain vs Saudi Arabia at World Cup 2026: TV channel, how to watch, kick-off time, live stream, referee, predicted line-ups - ESPN

Skip to main content Skip to navigation

play

Spain look to get their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign back on track when they take on Saudi Arabia in a Group H encounter at Atlanta.

Luis de la Fuente's side bore plenty of criticism after a 0-0 draw with Cape Verde in their World Cup opener, but have been channeling the spirit of 2010, when Spain lost their first match en-route winning the World Cup.

Saudi Arabia, meanwhile, raised hopes of qualification to the knockout stages after a 1-1 draw with Uruguay.

Here's everything you need to know about Monday's game.

## How to watch:

The match will be available on BBC One in the UK, Fox Sports in the U.S., Zee5 in India and SBS in Australia. You can also follow ESPN's live updates.

## Key Details:

Date, kick-off time: U.S. ET: Sunday June 21, 12 p.m. UK BST: Sunday June 21, 5 p.m. India IST: Sunday June 21, 9.30 p.m. Australia AEST: Monday June 22, 2 a.m.

Venue: Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta, USA Referee: Raphael Claus (Brazil)

## Team News and Predicted line-ups:

Spain
